memoryArtifacts.headline: Thomas Master - notes
memoryArtifacts.description:
Notes

 
Thomas Master, of Wye, Kent, Eng., is the first proven ancestor of this family. He m. Agnes _____ and died in 1509. His will was probated 21 Apr. 1509, giving bequests to the church and askin …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Masters III於1442年出生在英國英格蘭肯特維也時，他的父親Thomas Masters II25歲，母親Denyse21歲。他於1462年與Agnes Webbe在英國肯特維也結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1509年4月21日在他的家鄉享年67歲。

nameMeanings.name: Masters
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational and occupational name from Middle English (atte) maisteres ‘(at the) master's (house)’, a name for someone who lived or worked there, normally a servant. Compare Master and Masterman , and for the type compare Maidens , Monks , Parsons .
variant of Master , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Master
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Amar, Dhimant, Pravin, Amit, Aruna, Bhanu, Gaurang, Gopal, Haren, Jagu, Jayesh, Kalpesh.

English: occupational name from Middle English maister ‘master’ (Old French maistre). The word had a wide range of applications, and the reference in surnames may be to a schoolmaster, to the master of a farm or house, to the master of an apprentice, or to a ship's captain. In Scotland the eldest sons of barons and the uncles of lords were called Masters, but this is not primarily a Scottish surname.
German: dialect form of   Meister   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Maidens
meanings: occupational or habitational name from Middle English (atte) maydenes ‘(at the) maiden's or maidens' (house)’, a name for someone who lived or worked at such a house. Compare Masters and Parsons .
post-medieval variant of Maiden , with excrescent -s.

variants.name: Marsters
meanings: English: variant of Masters . Marsters is the predominant surname on Palmerston Island, Cook Islands, South Pacific, deriving from a settler and owner reputedly born in Leicestershire.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Masters III was born in 1442, in Wye, Kent, England, United Kingdom, his father, Thomas Masters II, was 25 and his mother, Denyse, was 21. He married Agnes Webbe in 1462, in Wye, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 21 April 1509, in his hometown, at the age of 67.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Masters III est né en 1442, à Wye, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Masters II, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Denyse, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Agnes Webbe en 1462, à Wye, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 21 avril 1509, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 67 ans.

Er hat 1462 in Wye, Kent, England Agnes Webbe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 21. April 1509 im Alter von 67 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Masters III(이)가 1442년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 윈예에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Masters II(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Denyse(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1462년 영국 켄트 윈예에서 Agnes Webbe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1509년 4월 21일 그의 고향에서 67세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes Webbe в 1462 г. в местности ви, кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер 21 апреля 1509 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 67 лет.
